UpdateConfigRequest.description:
Request to update app configuration.

``values`` may be plain strings (scalar fields) or multi-line YAML strings
(yaml_file fields). We type it ``Dict[str, Any]`` so multi-line YAML
survives Pydantic, but a ``field_validator`` enforces that each value is
actually a scalar (str / int / float / bool / None) — dicts and lists
would silently get ``str(v)``-ified into env vars (``"{'a': 1}"``) and
pollute the manifest JSON.
UpdateConfigRequest.title: UpdateConfigRequest

info.description:
IoT Solution Provisioning Platform for Seeed Studio products.

**Authentication:** Localhost requests are always allowed. Remote requests require an API key via `X-API-Key` header or `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.
info.title: Provisioning Station

get.description: Get current page state and recent navigation history.

post.description: Frontend reports current page navigation.
AI sends a command for the frontend to execute.

get.description: Frontend polls and takes pending commands (clears queue after read).

Aggregated AI context: page state + deployments + streams.

Returns a lightweight summary (~2-3KB). For details, use dedicated endpoints:
- Logs: GET /api/deployments/{id}/logs
- MQTT messages: GET /api/ai/streams?include_messages=true

Get AI interaction settings.

Returns:
- interaction_mode: "ui" (user sees page operations) or "background" (silent API calls)

External AI agents should call this endpoint to determine how to interact
with SenseCraft UI when using smart_deploy or similar tools.

put.description:
Update AI interaction settings.

External AI agents or users can change the interaction mode:
- "ui": All operations are visible in the UI (animations, page transitions)
- "background": Operations run silently without UI changes

get.description: Get active video streams and MQTT subscriptions.

Used by the frontend to test HTTP endpoints on deployed services
without running into CORS restrictions.

Download and install a specific solution update.

get.description: Get download progress for active downloads.

Return the host (and deploy method) for the given step.

Response shape:
    {
        "host": "<ip-or-hostname>" | "localhost",
        "device_type": "docker_local" | "docker_remote" | null,
        "source": "manifest" | "local_default"
    }

Status codes:
    200 — host resolved
    404 — manifest missing AND deploy_type cannot be inferred (no
          local fallback available)

Notes
-----
* Both query params are required; missing values produce 422 (FastAPI default).
* We deliberately do not pierce ``..`` etc. — the path is composed from
  validated identifiers and never echoed back to the client raw.

Delete the edit overlay for *sid* and reload the SolutionManager.

Idempotent — returns ok=True even if no edit session existed.

Seed the edit overlay directory for a solution.

Copies from the effective source (updates overlay if present, else bundled).
No-op (seeded: False) if the edit dir already exists.
Triggers a SolutionManager reload so the overlay becomes active immediately.
Returns 404 if the source solution is not found.

Package the edit overlay for *sid* into a zip file and return its path.

Archive entries are named ``{sid}/<relative/path>`` so that unzipping over
the git repo's ``solutions/`` directory drops content into the right place.
Returns 404 if no edit session exists for the given sid.

delete.description:
Delete an image from the edit overlay for a solution.

The ``path`` field must start with ``intro/gallery/`` or ``deploy/sections/``.
Returns ok=True whether or not the file existed.

post.description:
Upload an image into the edit overlay for a solution.

Constraints:
- content_type must be image/png, image/jpeg, or image/webp
- target_path must end with .png, .jpg, .jpeg, or .webp
- target_path must start with intro/gallery/ or deploy/sections/
- file size must be ≤ 5 MB

put.description:
Write markdown content to the edit overlay for a solution.

The ``file`` field must end with ``.md`` and start with ``intro/`` or
``deploy/``.  Path traversal attempts are rejected.  Parent directories
are created automatically.

get.description: Return the current state of the edit overlay directory for a solution.

put.description:
Apply a whitelisted patch to the edit overlay YAML for a solution.

Loads the current edit YAML, applies the patch (silently dropping
disallowed fields), validates the result with the Pydantic Solution model,
then saves.  On validation failure the file is still saved so the user
doesn't lose work, and a 422 is raised with the validation errors.
Returns 404 if no edit session exists for the given sid.

get.description:
Reverse-proxy ``GET /observation`` from the target device.

The frontend should resolve template placeholders before invoking this
endpoint when it has the host (e.g. user manually entered it). If the
template ``{{deploy.host}}`` cannot be resolved client-side (e.g. the
wizard was closed and reopened), pass ``solution_id`` + ``device_id``
so the backend renders the URL from the persisted deployment manifest.

parameters.description: Upstream URL (may contain {{deploy.host}} if solution_id+device_id provided)
parameters.name: endpoint

schema.description: Upstream URL (may contain {{deploy.host}} if solution_id+device_id provided)
schema.title: Endpoint
Response Proxy Observation Api Verify Robot Inspect Observation Get

post.description:
Generic L2 reverse proxy for action_modules verify-panel buttons.

Request body:
    {
        "solution_id": str,
        "device_id": str,
        "endpoint_template": str,   # MUST match an allowlisted template
        "method": "GET"|"POST",
        "payload": {...}             # optional, JSON-serialisable
    }

See module-level comment above for the validation chain. Any field
named ``render_vars`` in the request body is *silently ignored*: render
variables are rebuilt server-side from the deployment manifest.

get.description:
Reverse-proxy ``GET /observation/schema`` from the target device.

See :func:`proxy_observation` for ``solution_id`` / ``device_id``
fallback semantics.

parameters.description: Upstream URL (may contain {{deploy.host}} if solution_id+device_id provided)
parameters.name: endpoint

schema.description: Upstream URL (may contain {{deploy.host}} if solution_id+device_id provided)
schema.title: Endpoint
Response Proxy Schema Api Verify Robot Inspect Schema Get

get.description: Proxy health check to the Jetson speech service.

post.description:
Streaming proxy for TTS.

Proxies to Jetson /tts/stream which returns raw PCM (16-bit signed LE, mono).
The stream starts with a 4-byte little-endian uint32 sample rate header,
followed by raw PCM audio data. This proxy strips the header and streams
pure PCM data to the frontend.
